what_it_does: Defines an OXLint adherence configuration for a design system, including React/import plugins, warning rules for restricted imports and raw design values, an index-file override, and x-omelette metadata for design tokens, token kinds, and font families.
observations: Uses the react and import plugins.
Configures react/forbid-elements as a warning with an empty forbid list.
Restricts imports from multiple app, asset, tweak panel, and UI kit internal paths with a message directing consumers to index.js.
Warns on raw hex colors, raw px values, and font-family declarations outside Sora, DM Sans, and JetBrains Mono via no-restricted-syntax selectors.
Includes an x-omelette block with an empty components object, a token inventory, token kind mapping, and allowed font families.
Token kinds cover color, shadow, spacing, font, and other.
Radius tokens are classified as spacing.
standouts: Combines lint enforcement with design-system inventory metadata in the same JSON file.
The restricted import message establishes index.js as the public component access point.
The token inventory includes semantic colors, layout sizes, typography, motion, shadows, zones, pigment tokens, and spatial/vi tokens.
conventions: Rules are warning-level rather than error-level.
Design-system constraints are expressed as lint selectors and restricted import patterns.
Design tokens use CSS custom property naming with -- prefixes.
Token metadata is kept in a separate x-omelette namespace.
Index files are treated as allowed aggregation points for otherwise restricted imports.
concerns: react/forbid-elements is configured with an empty forbid list, so the rule has no listed elements to forbid in this file.
The tokens array and tokenKinds object duplicate token names, creating a file-local synchronization point.
The broad app/** restricted import pattern overlaps with several narrower app subpaths listed alongside it.
notes: This file is a design-system adherence ledger: it both warns on local usage that bypasses tokens or public exports and records the available token surface.
questions: Is the empty react/forbid-elements forbid list intentional as a placeholder?
Are radius tokens intentionally classified under spacing?
Are the narrower app restricted import patterns kept for readability despite the broad app/** pattern?
purpose_vs_actual: The apparent purpose matches the content: it configures design-system adherence linting and records token/font metadata used by that adherence layer.
intention_signals: It seems intended to keep consumers using public design-system entry points and approved tokens/fonts while exposing a structured inventory of the design substrate for tooling.
novelty: The x-omelette metadata block makes the lint config also act as a token and font ledger.
contribution: Contributes a neutral adherence contract: public import boundaries, raw style-value warnings, allowed font families, and a structured map of design tokens with their kinds.
summary_for_embedding: OXLint JSON config that enforces design-system import and styling conventions while embedding x-omelette metadata for tokens, token kinds, and font families.
intention_for_embedding: This was for guiding code toward design-system public exports and tokenized visual values while making the design substrate inspectable by tooling.
